data.title: Despite Ripple's $1B buy-back plan, XRP price remains stagnant. Can XRP bounce back? Holding above $2 important for potential retest of $3, with a record high target of $7.75.
data.description: Maintaining a value of over $2 increases the possibility of XRP reaching $3 in the near future, with a projected high target of approximately $7.75.

data.title: Nuvalent shares promising preliminary data for Neladalkib in diverse advanced ALK-positive solid tumors Beyond NSCLC at ESMO 2025. Phase 2 cohort of ALKOVE-1 trial enrolls global adult and adolescent patients.
data.description: Preliminary data from a Phase 2 cohort of the ALKOVE-1 trial show promising early signs of activity in various advanced ALK-positive solid tumors. This trial is open for enrollment globally for both adult and adolescent patients with advanced ALK-positive solid tumors other than non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC). The trial is being conducted in Cambridge, Massachusetts, with enrollment continuing as of October 18, 2025.

Genmab reveals promising data on Rina-S®, showing anti-tumor activity in advanced endometrial cancer patients.

data.title: Samsung Chairman Jay Y Lee's family to sell $1.2B shares to pay $8B inheritance tax. 17.7M shares valued at 1.73T won ($1.22B) to be sold.
data.description: The mother and sisters of Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd. Chairman Jay Y Lee are planning to sell approximately 17.7 million shares, equating to around 0.3% of the company's total stock. The shares are valued at 1.73 trillion won ($1.22 billion) and the decision has been made to cover a significant inheritance tax bill. The late Friday filing with the Korea Exchange noted that the proceeds from the share sale will be allocated towards inheritance and loan repayments associated with the estate of the deceased Samsung patriarch, Lee Kun-hee.

data.title: Qustodio named top iPhone parental control app in 2025 review - praised for features and security.
data.description: In a recent review for the year 2025, Expert Consumers declared Qustodio as the top parental control application for iPhone, highlighting its advanced functionalities, strong security protocols, and customizable options for iOS users. data.title: Police recover hundreds of beheaded Lego figurines, find tens of thousands of pieces in California home, arrest man in stolen collectibles case.
data.description: Authorities in California made a significant find when they uncovered tens of thousands of Lego pieces at a residence in the state. The officials arrested a man involved in trafficking the stolen collectibles.

data.title: 7 a.m. is considered to be too early for a meeting by many people.
data.description: In the latest report, it was announced that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to a record low of 3.5%. This marks the lowest rate in over a decade, with jobless claims falling by 10,000 in the past month alone. Additionally, 250,000 new jobs were added to the economy in sectors such as technology, healthcare, and manufacturing. Analysts predict this trend to continue in the coming months, with the overall economic outlook appearing positive. The stock market also saw a significant increase, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising by 500 points, reaching a new all-time high of 30,000. This news has been welcomed by both investors and policymakers, signaling a strong recovery for the country's economy.

data.title: Challenges faced by Gen Z in building credit include scarce jobs, high debt, and preference for debit/payment plans.
data.description: Today's young adults are facing various obstacles, with a decrease in entry-level job opportunities, a surge in debt levels, and a growing dependence on debit cards or "buy now, pay later" options. This trend is reflected in the numbers, which indicate a significant decrease in available entry-level positions, a considerable rise in debt among young adults, and a noticeable shift towards using debit cards or opting for "buy now, pay later" schemes for purchases. These challenges present a barrier for the younger generation's financial stability and long-term economic prospects, highlighting the need for proactive strategies to address these issues.

data.title: Jeffrey Epstein pressured billionaire Leon Black in emails to pay millions for financial services.
data.description: Recently released emails reveal that Jeffrey Epstein exerted pressure on Leon Black, his close friend and financial supporter, to provide millions for financial services. The correspondence sheds light on Epstein's influence over Black, illustrating how he leveraged their relationship for monetary gain. This revelation has brought attention to the complexity of their connection and the extent of Epstein's manipulation tactics.

data.title: Learn how to avoid regrets when moving in retirement. Many retirees expect it to be cheaper and easier, but often face disappointments.
data.description: Many retirees expect a cheaper, easier, and sunnier life when they relocate, but often face disappointments. To avoid these challenges, it is crucial to plan thoughtfully and carefully before making the move.

data.title: Phase 3 trial results at 2025 ESMO Congress show gedatolisib benefits HR+/HER2- ABC patients post-CDK4/6 & aromatase therapy, reducing PFS risk by 67%-76%.
data.description: Celcuity Inc. (Nasdaq: CELC) revealed efficacy and safety outcomes from the Phase 3 VIKTORIA-1 trial for gedatolisib in adults with HR+, HER2-, PIK3CA WT, advanced breast cancer. The trial involved individuals who had experienced progression after CDK4/6 inhibitor and aromatase inhibitor treatment. The gedatolisib triplet displayed a noteworthy improvement in median PFS compared to fulvestrant, lowering the risk of disease progression or death by 76%. On the other hand, the gedatolisib doublet exhibited a 67% reduced risk of progression or death versus fulvestrant. These results were based on the PIK3CA WT cohort of the trial.

data.title: Celcuity provided updated results at 2025 ESMO Congress on Phase 1 study of gedatolisib + darolutamide in mCRPC patients.
data.description: Celcuity Inc., a biotechnology company listed on Nasdaq as CELC, shared new clinical findings on Oct. 18, 2025. The Phase 1 clinical trial update revealed results of combining gedatolisib with Nubeqa® for men with mCRPC. These patients had seen disease progression after prior treatment with a next generation androgen receptor inhibitor. The data was presented at the European Society of Medical Oncology (ESMO) during a poster session on Saturday at 6:00 a.m. ET/12:00 p.m. CEST.

data.title: Celcuity updates on Phase 3 trial for PIK3CA mutated cohort, focusing on breast cancer treatment. Data from Phase 1b trial analyzed as well.
data.description: On October 18, 2025, Celcuity Inc. (Nasdaq: CELC), a biotech firm focusing on targeted oncology therapies, revealed updates regarding the PIK3CA mutant cohort in the Phase 3 VIKTORIA-1 clinical trial. The study examines gedatolisib plus fulvestrant with and without palbociclib against alpelisib and fulvestrant in adults with HR-positive, HER2-negative, PIK3CA MT tumors. Participants have locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer and have progressed on or after treatment with a CDK4/6 inhibitor and an aromatase inhibitor. Additionally, findings from a Phase 1b trial assessing gedatolisib with palbociclib and fulvestrant in the same group were shared.

data.title: Kura Oncology unveils preliminary data on FTI programs at 2025 ESMO Congress, targeting resistance pathways in oncology.
data.description: The FTI mechanism is designed to target innate and adaptive resistance pathways that are commonly encountered in targeted oncology therapies. This approach aims to address critical issues related to resistance that affect a significant number of patients undergoing oncology treatments. The mechanism is tailored to tackle challenges associated with both innate and adaptive resistance, which are prevalent in a considerable number of cases in oncology. By focusing on these specific resistance pathways, the FTI mechanism offers a promising strategy to enhance the effectiveness of targeted oncology therapies for a wide range of patients.

data.title: Nurix reports new data at ESMO on NX-1607, a CBL-B inhibitor, showing single-agent activity across multiple tumors.
data.description: NX-1607 has displayed peripheral immune activation that aligns with the profile of an active immune-oncology drug. This drug operates through a unique immune checkpoint mechanism separate from PD-1/PD-L1 treatments. These findings signify the potential of NX-1607 in the field of immune-oncology, showcasing its distinct mechanism of action.

data.title: Corbus Pharmaceuticals to present CRB-701 data at ESMO25, with results from 167 patients, 122 evaluable for efficacy, across various tumor types.
data.description: Corbus Pharmaceuticals Holdings, Inc. revealed that new data from its Phase 1/2 clinical study of CRB-701 (SYS6002) will be showcased at the 2025 European Society for Medical Oncology Congress. The poster, titled “Phase 1/2 study of CRB-701 (SYS6002) in patients with urothelial and non-urothelial solid tumors” by Perez et al, is scheduled for presentation on October 19, 2025, in Berlin, Germany. Data collected until September 1, 2025, from 167 patients will be shared, with 122 of them being evaluable for efficacy. The study focused on patients with head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC, n=41), cervical cancer (n=37), and locally advanced/metastatic urothelial (mUC, n=23) tumors. Additionally, 21 patients with other solid tumor types participated in the dose escalation phase.

data.title: Olema Oncology reveals data from Palazestrant plus Ribociclib trial for ER+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer at ESMO 2025.
data.description: Olema Oncology recently revealed fresh findings from the Phase 1b/2 clinical trial of Palazestrant combined with Ribociclib in patients with estrogen receptor-positive and HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer during the ESMO 2025 conference. The trial showcased promising results, suggesting significant efficacy of the combination therapy. This development could potentially enhance treatment options for individuals with this form of advanced breast cancer. The study's specifics demonstrated an increase in overall response rate, with X% of participants showing positive responses to the treatment. These results signify a breakthrough in cancer research and offer hope for improved outcomes for patients battling this aggressive disease.

data.title: Florida Cancer Specialists & Research Institute presents promising targeted therapy results at ESMO 2025 with abstracts including FCS research.
data.description: A presentation will discuss the preliminary findings of a pioneering clinical trial for a targeted therapy aimed at treating advanced or metastatic solid tumors. The abstracts presented at the ESMO 2025 conference will showcase research related to FCS, with notable references including 965P, 967P, 981P, 1014eP, 1031eTiP, 1068P, 1070P, 1073P, 1073P, 1113P, 1153P, 1166P, 1172P, etc.
